title: 'A real honour' - Jessie Buckley wins Golden Globe
text: Irish actress Jessie Buckley won Best Actress at the Golden Globes for her role in Hamnet, the historical drama adapted from Maggie O'Farrell's bestselling novel. The film also claimed the Best Picture, Drama award. During her acceptance speech at the Beverly Hilton in Los Angeles, Buckley expressed gratitude to director Chloé Zhao, co-star Paul Mescal, and the diverse production team. She stated: This is a real, real honour. I love what I do. The Killarney-born performer also praised her fellow nominees, including Julia Roberts and Jennifer Lawrence, calling them heroes and expressing admiration for their work. Hamnet tells the story of Agnes and William Shakespeare's lost son. Other major winners at the ceremony included the Netflix crime drama Adolescence and the comedy thriller One Battle After Another, each securing four awards.
